title: Climate Change
question: How much do our activities contribute to global warming?
guidance: Switch to renewable electricity, identify your main emission hotspots, and redesign products and logistics for lower carbon intensity. Use basic CCF and PCF tracking to support climate-aligned planning.

title: Pollution
question: Do our processes cause significant environmental pollution?
guidance: Identify pollution and waste sources early, replace hazardous inputs where feasible, and use certified waste or recycling partners. Track waste, recycling, and material-intensity KPIs.

title: Water
question: Do we threaten local ecosystems through water withdrawal?
guidance: Create a clear view of operational and supplier water use, add simple monitoring, and prioritise low-water alternatives or reuse options where possible.

title: Biodiversity
question: Do our activities destroy ecosystems or biodiversity (both fauna and flora)?
guidance: Check sourcing and site locations for biodiversity sensitivity, prefer certified responsible materials, and integrate biodiversity criteria into supplier and location decisions.

title: Circular Economy
question: How intensive is our use of resources and waste production?
guidance: Map material and waste flows, design for repair and separability, and track simple circularity KPIs such as recycled content, waste reduction, or reusable components.

title: Own Workforce
question: Do our practices violate employee rights?
guidance: Keep labour practices explicit from the start with safe working conditions, fair treatment, basic wellbeing checks, and a clear feedback channel for the team.

title: Supply Chain
question: Are there risks of labor violations in our value chain?
guidance: Use a lightweight supplier code of conduct, flag higher-risk regions or materials, and require basic transparency on labour and safety practices from key suppliers.

title: Communities
question: Do our actions impact local communities negatively?
guidance: Engage early with local stakeholders, keep communication open, and show practical local value creation through jobs, partnerships, or low-disruption operations.

title: Consumers
question: Do our products or services harm consumer rights?
guidance: Use clear claims, stronger QA, transparent pricing, and accessible support so product safety, fairness, and consumer trust are built in from the first launch.

title: Corporate Ethics
question: Do poor standards cause ethical violations in our company?
guidance: Assign responsibility for governance basics, keep records clean, define clear review and escalation rules, and establish simple anti-corruption and whistleblowing expectations.
